---
title: "Unit 0 — Tooling & Reproducibility"
subtitle: "GitHub Classroom · pytest · the SOLUTION-marker workflow · the seed policy"
engine: markdown
---

## Overview

Before any modelling, you need a pipeline that just works. Unit 0 gets you set up
end-to-end — clone the repo from GitHub Classroom, run the autograder with `pytest`, fill in a
function behind the SOLUTION markers, render the unit, and commit and push. Nothing here is
conceptually hard; the goal is a working toolchain and one habit — **seed every random number
generator** — that every later unit assumes.

## Learning objectives

By the end of Unit 0 you will be able to:

- **Run the autograder and fill a function** — implement `add` so `pytest` passes.
- **Reproduce a random draw** — implement `seeded_draw` so the same seed gives the same
  result (the *seed policy*).
- **Use the math/LaTeX toolchain** — write the power-rule derivative and complete the week-1
  checklist.
- **Use the Git/Classroom workflow** — clone, commit, and push your work.

## What you'll do

Work through four small setup tasks:

- **(1)** Implement `add` and confirm the autograder runs.
- **(2)** Implement `seeded_draw` so a fixed seed reproduces the same draw — this is the
  **seed policy** in one line.
- **(3)** Complete the onboarding exercise — the power-rule derivative
  $\frac{d}{dx}x^n = n\,x^{n-1}$ and the syllabus / academic-integrity checklist.
- **(4)** Clone, commit, and push through GitHub Classroom, leaving the `### SOLUTION ###`
  markers intact.

There is no model to build and nothing to interpret here — just a pipeline that runs clean.

## Prerequisites & connections

**Review first.** A working Python install and a GitHub account, plus basic terminal use
(clone, commit, push). No prior course content is assumed.

**Connections.**

> The **seed policy** you set up here is taught once and **assumed everywhere after** — Units
> 3 and 5 grade it directly as a Process spec. An unseeded RNG makes results irreproducible
> and un-gradeable, so build the habit now.

## How it's graded

Unit 0 is graded on **completion**, not correctness — it is the documented onboarding
exception to the course's usual three-bundle scheme (there is no model to gate and nothing to
interpret). Each of the four tasks is **complete / not-yet**, with one revise-and-resubmit,
and the unit passes when all four are done. U0 is a required **completion floor**: it does not
by itself raise your letter, but every grade above E depends on it. See
[`GRADING.md`](../GRADING.md) for how it fits the ladder.

## What to submit

- Your completed **`hw.py`** — `add` and `seeded_draw`, with the `### SOLUTION ###` markers
  left in place.
- Your **onboarding work** — the power-rule derivative and the ticked week-1 checklist, in the
  rendered unit.
- Your **pushed repository** — committed and pushed through GitHub Classroom.

*Submit via the course page — [submit mechanism TBD].*

## Deadline & revision window

- Due: **[PLACEHOLDER]**
- Post-feedback revision window: **[PLACEHOLDER]**

## Estimated effort

Roughly **1–2 hours [PROPOSED]** — this is setup, not analysis; most of the time goes into
installing the toolchain and confirming that the autograder and the push both work.
